blurbs.title: Barolo
blurbs.body: Castello Falletti and the WiMu wine museum, walkable cobble streets between four enotecas. Lunch at La Cantinetta. Buy the Cannubi at the cellar, not the gift shop.

blurbs.title: La Morra
blurbs.body: Belvedere terrace at Piazza Castello frames the wide read across the Barolo MGAs. LeWitt's multicolor chapel exterior 5 min downhill; Vignaiolo for the long lunch.

blurbs.title: Monforte d'Alba
blurbs.body: Hilltop village south of Barolo, the post-cellar dinner pick — bollito misto cart, seven cuts and seven sauces at Della Posta in the Sant'Anna hamlet. Two hours, slow.

blurbs.title: Castiglione Falletto
blurbs.body: Stone hilltop fortress at the geographic center of the eleven Barolo communes; the vineyard view from the castle ramparts is the cleanest geography lesson in the Langhe.

blurbs.title: Serralunga d'Alba
blurbs.body: The most architecturally intact Langhe castle — narrow towers, original keep, FAI-run with timed tours. Furthest from Alba on this list; pair with a Cigliuti or Vigna Rionda cellar visit to make the drive earn its keep.

blurbs.title: Barbaresco
blurbs.body: Smaller, lighter sister to Barolo across the Tanaro. Enoteca Regionale del Barbaresco in the deconsecrated 1100s church; Antinè for the Michelin one-star midweek tasting.

blurbs.title: Treiso
blurbs.body: Nearest of the three Barbaresco communes to Alba. Pajè-cru vineyard ridge, Rivetto and Pelissero cellars, lunch at La Ciau del Tornavento for the Michelin-and-view register without the Castiglione drive.

blurbs.title: Neive
blurbs.body: Medieval upper village ringed by Barbaresco vineyards; Bottega dei Quattro Vini and the small Piedmontese cellars in the lower town. Quieter than Barolo on a weekday.

blurbs.title: Diano d'Alba
blurbs.body: The Dolcetto-first hilltop on the south rim of Alba. Sörì-system vineyard signage, panoramic terrace with the Maritime Alps on a clear day, weekday osteria lunches at La Briccola for under €40 per cover.

blurbs.title: Grinzane Cavour
blurbs.body: 11th-century castle holding Cavour's apartments and the Enoteca Regionale Piemontese. Closed Tuesdays year-round per the official tickets page; v3 anchors it on Day 4 Thursday morning before the Liguria transit.
